ranked_scenarios.summary: Rename Select Mode to Transform Mode, and create a new Select Mode without transform gizmo

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

Closes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/7834

Related: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/87756

Currently bound to `V`, open to suggestions. This can be changed to be anything by modifying shortcuts in the editor, in case people want this to be the option bound to `Q`. 

Region selection works even when initiated on top of a node, which is another benefit of this mode. 

Edit: See below video for updated icon. 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2471cd14-51b3-4a37-9c89-5cadaa64c389

Thanks to @didrif for providing the SVG for the transform tool. 

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b47e3250-dbea-4f11-8977-f1081ea1ab81)

ranked_scenarios.summary: UX and stability improvements for `Path3D` node

description.text:
UX and stability improvements for `Path3D` node.

Goals are:
* Declutter the UI showing only the appropriate handles in the respective modes;
* Solving issues caused by subgizmos overlapping each other.

Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/89138
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/85795
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/88276 offering a "cleaner" solution
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/8890

TODOs:
- [x] Show transform gizmo only when in `Select Points` mode;
- [x] Show secondary handles only when in `Select Control Points` mode;
- [x] Clear subgizmo selection when changing mode;
- [x] Add a `Select Tilt Points` mode that only shows tilt disks.

Toolbar with the new `Select Tilt Points` mode:
![immagine](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/62719360/e5c24572-1432-418c-8c96-701e328546e4)

`Select Points` mode is cleaner (the only thing is missing is changing the color of the selected control points, eventually suggest in which part of the code this change should be made, I couldn't get it in a straightforward way):

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/62719360/4a6917e8-6da3-4ad6-8d58-a5a897e61c84

`Select Control Points` shows the secondary handles. This mode permits moving points freely. If you need to "drag out" the points from their default position, you have to press shift (the tooltip text explains that):

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/62719360/6d703977-5166-42d3-9a58-b6b3accf0c79

`Select Tilt Points` is the new mode that shows the tilt disks and handles, decluttering the other modes and solving other issues (tilt handles and secondary handles where often in conflict):

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/62719360/264ff7a8-111e-48cd-8596-fa74c548a9ce

closed_issues.summary: Path3D gizmo: Missing Bezier curve handles for the points

closed_issues.summary: 4.2 - Path3D Control Nodes - Cannot create bezier handles in top down ortho view.

description.text:
### Tested versions

works correctly in v4.1.3.stable.official [f06b6836a] as it doesnt have the Tilt Handles in editor.

issue occurs in v4.2.stable.official [46dc27791] as tilt handles were introduced.

### System information

Godot v4.2.stable - Windows 10.0.22621 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.4592) - AMD Ryzen 5 4600H with Radeon Graphics (12 Threads)

### Issue description

with the path3d node and looking in top down orthgraphic view cannot use control point button to drag and create bezier handles.
only the tilt handle is selectable due to it being higher up and making the control point unclickable to drag.

### Steps to reproduce

all i done was in 4.2 was create a new 3d scene.
create a path3d node.
into top down ortho view.
created a few points with the ![image](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/15170625/ce3d4b54-2909-4272-bf1c-5fe88f5b2c8b) button.

then, using the select control points button 
![image](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/15170625/bae00fdb-b25b-43cd-b9d0-d0ee37c9f6a0)
to try and create bezier handles.

this only indicates that the tilt handle has been selected and cannot create beziers.

my only workaround was to look from bottom up ortho, create the bezier handles and then return to top down ortho view to adjust placements.

possible solution would maybe have another button that hides or only controls the tilt handles

![image](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/15170625/1916548f-fdf4-4cf8-bfe7-4bf0247ac476)


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

ranked_scenarios.summary: Replace Ctrl in editor shortcuts with Cmd or Ctrl depending on platform

description.text:
following #64021 
related issue #64456 (Fix the issue but the issue is for 3.5 so not really a fix to this issue)

After a thorough check of all instances where CTRL is used(`is_ctrl_pressed()`,`Key::CTRL` and `KeyModifierMask::CTRL`) i have sorted the uses in multiple categories here in this PR are three categories where i think modifications should be done : 
- the first commit consist on modification that aims for better readibility and a clearer code. This is mostly replacing places where `CMD_OR_CTRL` was reimplemented on the fly and remove him from places where he was checked in conjonction with both `CTRL` and `META`
- the second commit consist on modification that are needed because the use of `CTRL` is either preventing MacOS user from using the functionality or makes it inconsistent with either what is said or what is already done else where.
- the last commit is about modified actions for dropping when `CTRL` is pressed. This functionality is used consistently with ctrl but on mac. it seems `CMD` would be more appropriate especially since you might start pressing the modifier button when starting to drag.

There are a few other places where `CTRL` is used and i think it should stay like this but i'm not entirely sure [see here for all places](https://github.com/ajreckof/godot/compare/change_CTRL_for_command_or_control...ajreckof:godot:change_ctrl_for_cmd_or_ctrl_what_to_keep?diff=unified):
- snapping : it is pretty clear when you press control it activates a snap. I think it should stay as `CTRL` but this is the one i'm the least sure of. 
- zoom with `CTRL+ SCROLL`: when scrolling pressing `CTRL` will enables you to zoom. This is better as `CTRL` as this is fundamentally not a MacOS way to do it. Therefore even on mac if i think of doing it i would use `CTRL` as if i was on a windows. 
- one case where pressing control would switch between scroll and pan movements. I was not sure about this one but it seemed similar to zooming as i would look for `CTRL` instead of `CMD`


The problem here is that i currently have only my experience and what feels right to me. So i would really like the opinion of other MacOS users on the subject. 

Left to do (maybe another PR): 
- [x] check all documentation linked to CMD_OR_CTRL to add it is `CMD` if it is not already done (edit : did everything in the editor part might do the documentation part later)
- [x] rename `is_command_or_control_pressed()` to `is_cmd_or_ctrl_pressed()`to be more in line with other similar functions (`is_ctrl_pressed()`)

ranked_scenarios.summary: Fix key mapping changes when moving from macOS to other platform.

description.text: - Removes separate `Command` key (use `Meta` instead). - Instead of it and `store_command` property, adds an event property to automatically remap `Command` ↔ `Control` (if set `Control` or `Meta` can't be set, and it is matched to `Control` or `Meta` depending on platform). - Uses platform specific key names on macOS ("Command" instead of "Meta", "Option" instead of "Alt"). Example: - First mapping is mapped to `Option+Command+Q` on macOS and to `Alt+Ctrl+Q` on other platforms. - Second is mapped to `Option+Command+A` on macOS and to `Alt+Meta+A` on other platforms. Fixes #35355

closed_issues.summary: Input map is changed when project is opened on macOS

description.text:
**Godot version:**
3.1, 3.2 RC2

**OS/device including version:**
Mac OS

**Issue description:**
When opening a project with an input bound to CTRL+(letter key), the binding automatically changes to CTRL+Coammand+(letter key). I would expect the input map to be unchanged.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Create a project on Linux or Windows with CTRL+g bound to an action (attached example).
2. Open the project on MacOS.
3. The keybinding is now change in the project.godot:
`diff --git a/project.godot b/project.godot
index a92f97e..c5ba98a 100644
--- a/project.godot
+++ b/project.godot
@@ -17,6 +17,6 @@ _global_script_class_icons={

 test_action={
 "deadzone": 0.5,
-"events": [ Object(InputEventKey,"resource_local_to_scene":false,"resource_name":"","device":0,"alt":false,"shift":false,"control":true,"meta":false,"command":true,"pressed":false,"scancode":71,"unicode":0,"echo":false,"script":null)
+"events": [ Object(InputEventKey,"resource_local_to_scene":false,"resource_name":"","device":0,"alt":false,"shift":false,"control":true,"meta":true,"command":true,"pressed":false,"scancode":71,"unicode":0,"echo":false,"script":null)
  ]
 }
`

**Minimal reproduction project:**
[input-test.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/4085789/input-test.zip)
input-test.zip contains a project with the changes Godot on MacOS produces in the unstaged chandes. A git hard reset brings back the project to the state before it was opened on Mac.
